mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near zero (0.19%) since late 2008, the persistently low policy rate compresses equity discount rates and supports risk asset valuations continuously, producing a structural upward drift in S&P 500 forward returns over quarterly horizons as long as the zero-bound regime persists.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive drift (t-test p > 0.10) during periods when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE remains elevated above 9.5% for consecutive months, as observed in the August 2010 reading of 9.6%, market participants price in a higher probability of additional Federal Reserve accommodation (e.g., QE2), which triggers a positive repricing of equities over the following month as easing expectations build.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 9.5% for 2 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS < 0.5%
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger is active, 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or not significantly different from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05) across at least two non-overlapping historical windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous deceleration in INDPRO growth and PAYEMS growth signals a broad-based loss of production and hiring momentum that jointly forecasts weaker corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month; neither series alone reliably predicts this outcome because production slowdowns without labor confirmation are often noise.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth < 1% AND PAYEMS 3-month change < 0
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised value 92.68 in August 2010 vintage showing deceleration from prior vintage
failure_condition: When both conditions are jointly triggered, 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances across the historical sample, invalidating the joint deceleration signal.

mechanism_narrative: In the fragile post-crisis housing recovery regime of 2010, continued weakness in HOUST (housing starts at only 546k, far below pre-crisis norms) signals ongoing balance-sheet repair and weak consumer demand, which drags on broader economic sentiment and depresses equity returns over a quarterly horizon specifically during this fragile recovery phase.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 546k units, July 2010 vintage, near post-crisis lows
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are positive and statistically indistinguishable from unconditional average (p > 0.10) during periods of sustained low HOUST readings below 600k, rejecting the depressive housing-drag mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation falls below the Fed's implicit comfort zone (roughly under 1.5%), disinflationary pressure raises expectations of additional quantitative easing, which is priced by equity markets as a positive liquidity signal over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.605 revised value, July 2010, consistent with sub-2% YoY inflation
failure_condition: During periods when CPIAUCSL YoY growth is below 1.5%, 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month changes in PAYEMS directly reflect the pace of labor market improvement or deterioration, and positive payroll surprises produce an immediate short-horizon rally in equities as investors update growth expectations within the following trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130415k revised value, August 2010 vintage
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS month-over-month prints are not significantly higher than following negative prints (t-test p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Joint weakness in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) signals a synchronized contraction in both discretionary consumption and housing-linked demand, which together forecast a broader consumer demand shortfall that depresses corporate revenue expectations and S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon; individually each series is too noisy to carry this signal.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised value 349188M showing downward revision from vintage 363704M in August 2010
regime_hint: weak consumer and housing demand co-movement
failure_condition: When both joint conditions are met, 60-day forward SPX returns exceed the unconditional historical mean in more than 55% of instances, invalidating the joint demand-weakness mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle economic recovery regime following a deep recession, positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO signals resumption of manufacturing capacity utilization that leads equity markets to price in earnings recovery, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following month; this relationship is regime-specific to recovery phases and weakens in late-cycle expansions.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 92.6805 revised value, August 2010 vintage, still below pre-crisis levels
failure_condition: During periods identified as early-cycle recovery, 20-day forward returns following positive INDPRO growth are not significantly higher than baseline (p > 0.10), or the effect reverses sign in more than one historical recovery episode.
